theme_notes: PSG won back-to-back Champions League titles by beating Arsenal 4-3 on penalties in Budapest (Gabriel Magalhães missed the decider); the US-Iran war has nearly closed the Strait of Hormuz, grinding global shipping; and Trump's administration is floating a $250 commemorative bill with his face on it for America's 250th anniversary.
settled: You're square. The Strait of Hormuz cannot say the same.
Balanced. Gabriel Magalhães is still working on that.
Even. More stable than the US-Iran ceasefire, which is nothing.
youOwe: Less than the fee to reroute one oil tanker around the Strait of Hormuz. Pathetic.
Arsenal blew the UCL final on penalties. You blew less than fifty bucks on what?
Not enough to print a commemorative $250 Trump bill. You're below denomination.
This is embarrassing money. Gabriel Magalhães embarrassed himself for millions, for free.
You could fund one back-channel diplomatic text to Tehran. Instead: nothing.
Pope Leo XIV called the Iran war unjust. He'd call this debt a spiritual emergency.
Arsenal led in the 5th minute and still lost. You've been losing this for longer.
Enough to buy a commemorative $250 bill with change left. Congratulations on that bar.
The Strait of Hormuz is blocked. So is your sense of urgency about any of this.
PSG won back-to-back. You've been behind back-to-back months.
Iran closed the Strait. Your wallet pulled the same stunt.
Congress debates Trump's face on currency. We debate your face at dinner.
Arsenal bottled the final. You've been bottling this conversation for weeks.
Global shippers are rerouting entire fleets. This debt is your Strait of Hormuz.
The House bipartisanly rebuked Trump's war powers. Your finances face the same fate.
Iran fully blocked the Strait. You've fully blocked repayment.
PSG: back-to-back titles. You: back-to-back missed windows. Only one is impressive.
The US and Iran are actively at war. Still a more functional relationship than your finances.
theyOwe: They owe you less than Arsenal's crisis PR budget for last Saturday. Collect anyway.
Forty bucks. Before the Strait crisis, this bought oil. Now it buys resentment.
A rounding error to them. A personality trait to you.
Gabriel Magalhães let down millions. They let you down for under two hundred. Same energy.
Dembélé equalized in the 66th minute. They haven't made up anything in months.
The Pope called the Iran war unjust. File this debt under the same category.
Arsenal led, then folded. They had the money and folded the same way.
Ships are rerouting around the Strait of Hormuz. You're rerouting around their texts.
Trump wants his face on the $250 bill. You want your money back. One of you is reasonable.
Arsenal needed extra time just to tie. You've needed extra months just for a response.
Iran closed the Strait. They closed their Venmo to you specifically.
The House voted to rein in Trump's war powers. Time to rein in their spending.
PSG won two UCL titles in a row. They've won two years of your patience.
Companies rerouted entire shipping lanes around the Strait. Reroute around this person.
The $250 Trump bill feels less absurd than this balance. And that's saying something.
Iran blocked the Strait of Hormuz. They blocked the door on ever paying you back.
PSG went back-to-back champion. They went ghost-to-ghost debtor.
The US and Iran are closer to a nuclear deal than you are to seeing this money.
